_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 73-77 (state tier). Mehta (HD-73,D), Cherry (HD-74,R), Dougherty (HD-75,D), Gardner (HD-76,D), Schmidt (HD-77,D). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll calls (VA LIS vote-detail pages, 2026 Reg. Session) + issue scorecards (ReproRising/Choice Tracker, Giffords/Everytown) + sponsored bills + documented campaign-platform statements. The three 2026 marquee constitutional amendments converge across independent agents on the LIS vote-detail pages: HJ1 reproductive-freedom (House 64-34), HJ2 felon voting restoration (65-33), HJ3 marriage (67-31), all 2nd reference, Jan 2026. Cherry is the lone Republican here: NAY on HJ1 AND HJ3 (no cross-party deviation; a false secondary claim that he voted YES on marriage was debunked against the LIS HJ3 roll call). The four Democrats voted/sponsored the progressive slate uniformly. Schmidt is a freshman seated Jan 17 2026 (special-election winner) who MISSED the Jan 16 amendment votes; his record rests on sponsored bills + documented platform positions, flagged in his note. reset_unspecified clears prior party-default guesses to null; only documented cells scored.

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND (not documented; not inferred)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-73 (part of Chesterfield County); freshman, assumed office Jan 14 2026 after defeating GOP incumbent Mark Earley Jr. Nov 4 2025. Attorney; former Legal Director of the ACLU of Virginia; interim CEO of the International Rett Syndrome Foundation. JD Howard University; BA UNC.

leslie-mehta.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-73 (Chesterfield); freshman seated Jan 2026; former ACLU-VA Legal Director. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record in her first session: VPAP shows 100% Democratic-caucus voting unity, 0 votes against caucus. Voted YEA and is a co-patron of HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, House 64-34); YEA on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage amendment, 67-31); YEA on HJ2 (felon voting restoration). Pro-IVF campaign platform. Endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. Full gun-control slate: YEA on the assault-firearms/large-capacity-magazine bans (HB1524/HB217), red-flag training (HB896), concealed-carry competence requirement (HB916); Giffords PAC endorsed. Voted to expand absentee voting/automatic registration and for parole/sentence-credit expansions. Voted YEA on HB1441 restricting local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement (sanctuary-leaning). No conservative-aligned documented position found. Christian-liberty and federal-overreach dimensions have no on-point recorded vote in her single session -> null, not inferred.

0.note: Voted YEA on and co-patron of HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34) -> does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Actively advocates the opposite of abortion abolition (chief co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment); pro-IVF platform.

2.note: Campaign centers on protecting IVF (conceived via IVF); does not oppose IVF/embryonic-research practices.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List (a PAC whose endorsement is financial backing) and Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia.

0.note: Voted YEA on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House 67-31) -> does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YEA to enshrine an affirmative right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted YEA on absentee-ballot expansion (HB773/HB774, HB82) and automatic voter registration (HB319) -> opposite of single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Voted to expand absentee/mail-in voting (cure process, receipt deadline) -> opposite of opposing mass mail-in voting.

1.note: Voted YEA on parole-eligibility expansions (HB1030/HB193/HB318), earned-sentence-credit (HB361), and juvenile-confinement limits (HB91) -> leniency direction, not tough-on-crime.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB1441/SB783 restricting local law-enforcement agreements with federal immigration authorities -> sanctuary-leaning, opposite of opposing sanctuary policies.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB916 adding a demonstrated-competence requirement for concealed-handgun permits -> opposite of constitutional/permitless carry.

1.note: Voted YEA on the assault-firearms + large-capacity-magazine ban (HB217/HB1524) and red-flag training (HB896).

profile.religion: Christian; staff pastor at Life Church (Chester, VA) and former Head of School at Life Christian Academy; B.S. in Religion & Biblical Studies (Liberty University), M.Ed./Ed.S. (Regent). Specific denomination not publicly documented (Life Church appears non-denominational).
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-74 (Colonial Heights + southern/central Chesterfield); first elected 2021 (old HD-66), moved to HD-74 after 2023 redistricting; re-elected Nov 4 2025. Retired U.S. Air Force Master Sergeant (1991-2011); former Colonial Heights City Council member; volunteer chaplain, Colonial Heights PD.

mike-cherry.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-74 (Colonial Heights/Chesterfield); Christian pastor + Air Force retiree; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record on the dimensions where evidence exists. Voted NAY on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment) -- verified on the LIS HJ1 roll call -- and NAY on the 2025 first-reference (HJR1/SJR247); rated 85%+ by the Family Foundation; voted YES on born-alive infant-protection bills; public post-Dobbs statement supporting return of abortion to the states. KEY DATA-QUALITY NOTE: voted NAY on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage amendment) -- the LIS HJ3 roll call lists him in the NAYS; a false secondary claim that he voted YES is debunked (the only House GOP marriage crossovers were Bloxom, Garrett, McNamara, NOT Cherry). Direct statements support school choice ('the only way') and well-funded/accountable police (volunteer police chaplain). Election-integrity, federal-overreach, immigration, and 2A specifics could not be isolated to his individual roll call (his sponsored work is largely bipartisan health/anti-trafficking), so left null pending a cited vote -- per the Durant precedent of not scoring caucus-unity inference.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, and 2025 HJR1/SJR247); rated 85%+ by the Family Foundation; voted YES on born-alive infant-protection bills; public statement that abortion should return to the states. Consistent pro-life record.

Voted NAY on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House 67-31; Cherry in the NAYS) -> affirmed marriage as currently defined, not same-sex marriage. (A false secondary claim of a YES vote was checked against the LIS roll call and rejected.)

1.note: Voted NAY on enshrining same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution -> opposes same-sex marriage in law.

0.note: Public statement: 'school choice is the only way. Parents should have the right to choose what educational model is best for their student.' Former Head of School at a private Christian academy.

Public platform backs a 'well funded, well trained' police system and preserving qualified immunity; serves as a volunteer police chaplain for the Colonial Heights PD -> affirmative support for law enforcement.

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND (not documented; not inferred)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-75 (parts of Chesterfield, Prince George, City of Hopewell); freshman, first elected Nov 4 2025 (defeated GOP incumbent Carrie Coyner), seated Jan 14 2026. Senior Research Administrator at Virginia Commonwealth University; BA University of Tennessee, MPA NC State.

lindsey-dougherty.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-75 (Chesterfield/Prince George/Hopewell); freshman seated Jan 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive HARD record (single 2026 session). Confirmed individual roll-call YEAs: HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment, HJ3 same-sex-marriage amendment, HJ2 felon voting restoration, HB1 ($15 minimum wage), and HB217 (assault-firearms + large-capacity-magazine ban). Co-patron of HB650 (limits federal immigration enforcement in 'protected areas,' sanctuary-style; vetoed by Gov. Spanberger) and HB1482 (police facial-covering ban). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia, ReproRising/Choice Tracker (Pro-Choice), and Giffords PAC. Her own chief-patron bills are local/administrative (textbooks, teacher exit-data, family-abuse protective orders) and do not touch the rubric. No cross-party deviation. Christian-liberty, family-sovereignty specifics, federal-overreach, and several other cells have no on-point recorded vote -> null, not inferred.

0.note: Voted YEA on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34) -> does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted for the reproductive-freedom amendment -> the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YEA on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment -> does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YEA to enshrine an affirmative right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Co-patron of HB650 restricting federal civil-immigration enforcement in 'protected areas' (sanctuary-style; passed House 63-35) -> opposite of opposing sanctuary policies.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB217 banning 'assault firearms' and large-capacity (15+ round) magazines.

profile.religion: Baptist (per published biography; no congregation named)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-76 (part of Chesterfield County); first elected 2023, re-elected Nov 4 2025. Career social worker; former Chief Deputy Director of the VA Dept. of Corrections; former Richmond Director of Social/Justice Services. MPA Virginia Commonwealth University; BA Sociology, NC Central. Chairs the HHS Behavioral Health subcommittee; sits on Public Safety (incl. Firearms subcommittee).

debra-gardner.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-76 (Chesterfield); Baptist; in office since 2024, re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive HARD record. Co-patron/sponsor of all three progressive constitutional amendments in the 2025 first reference: HJR1 (reproductive freedom), HJ9 (same-sex marriage), HJR2 (felon voting restoration) -- all passed the House on party-line votes she voted for as a co-patron. ReproRising Virginia graded her 'A'/Reproductive Freedom Leader. On guns, Giffords endorsed her on stated positions to ban assault weapons, strengthen the red-flag law, and improve background checks; Everytown spent ~$22.6k on her 2021 campaign. Patroned a tipped-wage/wage-theft study bill (HB335, vetoed). No cross-party deviation found. Family-sovereignty, Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, and immigration cells have no on-point cited record -> null, not inferred.

0.note: Co-patron of HJR1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, passed House 51-48); ReproRising 'A'/Reproductive Freedom Leader -> does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment -> the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ9, the amendment to repeal Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban and affirm a right to marry regardless of sex (passed House 58-35) -> does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Co-patron of the constitutional amendment enshrining same-sex marriage.
Giffords-endorsed on stated positions to ban assault weapons, strengthen the extreme-risk (red-flag) law, and improve background checks.

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND (not documented; not inferred)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-77 (southern City of Richmond + northern Chesterfield); freshman, won a Jan 6 2026 special election 76.5%-23.4% and sworn in Jan 17 2026. Civil-rights attorney; former attorney/advocate with the ACLU of Virginia; union member (SEIU Local 512, United Campus Workers); self-described progressive Democrat.

charlie-schmidt.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-77 (Richmond/Chesterfield); freshman seated Jan 17 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] RECORD CAVEAT: Schmidt was sworn in Jan 17, ONE DAY AFTER the Jan 16 second-reference votes on HJ1 (reproductive freedom) and HJ3 (marriage) -- he has NO roll call on either. His scoring therefore rests on HARD documented public-position evidence: his campaign platform (protect reproductive 'shield laws,' support the marriage-equality amendment, protect trans youth, oppose school choice/charters/vouchers, ban assault weapons, prohibit cooperation with federal civil immigration enforcement) and his chief-patron bills via Courts of Justice (limit qualified immunity, restrict civil/immigration-warrant enforcement in sensitive locations). Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia publicly welcomed his win. No roll-call or scorecard grade exists yet; cells without a documented position are null. No cross-party deviation -- platform is uniformly aligned with the Democratic caucus.

0.note: Campaign platform pledges to protect reproductive healthcare and enact 'shield laws' for abortion providers -> documented public position contrary to affirming personhood from conception. (Seated Jan 17, missed the Jan 16 HJ1 vote -> position from platform, not roll call.)

1.note: Platform protects abortion access -> the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Platform states he supports the constitutional amendment protecting marriage equality -> does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman. (Seated Jan 17, missed the Jan 16 HJ3 vote -> position from platform.)

2.note: Platform pledges to 'protect trans youth and the entire LGBTQ+ community' -> does not affirm biological sex as immutable; embraces transgender ideology in policy.

0.note: Platform opposes school choice: 'Stop the encroachment of charter schools, vouchers, and private enterprise in our public schools.'

2.note: Platform pledges to prohibit cooperation agreements with federal agents on civil immigration enforcement (sanctuary-style) and sponsored a bill restricting civil-warrant enforcement in sensitive locations -> opposite of opposing sanctuary policies.

1.note: Platform pledges to 'Ban assault weapons' and impose stronger gun-ownership licensing requirements.
